coredata.dc:title: Effect of La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier on primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys
coredata.prism:copyright: Copyright © 2005 Elsevier B.V. All rights reserved.
coredata.dc:creator: Xu, C.L.
Wang, H.Y.
Yang, Y.F.
Wang, Hong-Ying
Jiang, Q.C.
coredata.dc:description: The effect of La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier on primary silicon has been investigated. The results show that La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier play an important role in modifying primary silicon. According to the present experiment, when the modifier containing La2O3 was added to Al–20wt.% Si alloy at 850°C, La2O3 in the modifier was decomposed (partly or completely). The minimum primary silicon size (approximately average 18μm) modified with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier is obtained at the holding time of 30min, and the primary silicon size hardly change with the increase in holding time from 30 to 90min. Primary silicon size (approximately average 150μm) in unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy is about nine times larger than that (approximately average 18μm) in modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at the holding time of 30min. As compared with unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy, the Brinell hardness value of modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at the holding time of 30min is increased by 39%.
coredata.dcterms:subject: Primary silicon
Modification
Holding time
Hypereutectic Al–Si alloys

ce:caption.ce:simple-para: SEM micrograph of unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy.
SEM micrographs of modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier at different holding times: (a) 5min; (b) 30min; (c) 90min.
SEM micrographs of modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at different holding times: (a) 5min; (b) 15min; (c) 30min; (d) 90min; (e) 180min; (f) 240min.
Relation of primary silicon size with holding time after modification with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier.
XRD pattern of phase compositions of the sintered Al–10wt.% La2O3 compact (held at 850°C for 1h).
Chemical compositions of Al–20wt.% Si alloy (wt.%)
Effects of different modifiers and holding times on Brinell hardness

article.ce:title: Effect of La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier on primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys

ce:affiliation.ce:textfn: The Key Laboratory of Automobile Materials, Ministry of Education and Department of Materials Science and Engineering, Jilin University at Nanling Campus, No. 142 Renmin Street, Changchun 130025, PR China

ce:abstract-sec.ce:simple-para: The effect of La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier on primary silicon has been investigated. The results show that La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier play an important role in modifying primary silicon. According to the present experiment, when the modifier containing La2O3 was added to Al–20wt.% Si alloy at 850°C, La2O3 in the modifier was decomposed (partly or completely). The minimum primary silicon size (approximately average 18μm) modified with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier is obtained at the holding time of 30min, and the primary silicon size hardly change with the increase in holding time from 30 to 90min. Primary silicon size (approximately average 150μm) in unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy is about nine times larger than that (approximately average 18μm) in modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at the holding time of 30min. As compared with unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy, the Brinell hardness value of modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at the holding time of 30min is increased by 39%.

ce:section.ce:para: Considerable efforts have been devoted to the development of lightweight engineering materials during the last decades [1]. Recently, there is growing interest in hypereutectic Al–Si alloy as a candidate material instead of the conventional hypoeutectic Al–Si alloy. Al–Si alloys containing more than 15wt.% silicon are known as hypereutectic Al–Si alloys and have high-temperature strength, low expandability, high hardness, and high wear resistance [2,3]. Hypereutectic Al–Si alloys for making cylinders usually contains 16–26wt.% silicon and was divided into three groups: The first group contains 17–19wt.% silicon, the second group 20–23wt.% silicon, and the third group 24–26wt.% silicon. The first and second groups possess good properties and are widely used in industries. The third group is mainly used in the fabrication of high-speed automotive cylinders [4]. Under normal cast condition, primary silicon crystals in unmodified hypereutectic Al–Si alloys exhibit a variety of shapes such as polygonal, star-like (five-folded), coarse platelet, etc. [5]. If hypereutectic Al–Si alloys are not modified, coarse primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys usually results in the inferior strength [4] and surface roughness of machine parts.

One effective method to improve the machinability of hypereutectic Al–Si alloys is modification. Many of the studies have been carried out to modify primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys with modifiers containing phosphorus [6,7], Ti+B [8], calcium [9], strontium [10], sodium [11], boron [12], sulfur [4], rare earths (RE) [13–17]. But limited information is available regarding the effects of La2O3 and holding time on primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys. Therefore, an attempt has been made to design a new modifier containing La2O3 that can modify primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys. At the same time, the effect of holding time on primary silicon has been investigated after the modifier was added to the melt of hypereutectic Al–Si alloys. The variation of Brinell hardness of modified hypereutectic Al–Si alloys with primary silicon size has been also tested.

One modifier used in the experiment consists of Al, P, Ti, TiC and La2O3 (0.4P–10Ti–20TiC–12La2O3–Al balance, all in wt.%), and another consists of Al, P, Ti and TiC (0.4P–10Ti–20TiC–Al balance, all in wt.%). The chemical compositions of the base alloy are given in Table 1 . The base alloy was melted with a temperature of 850°C in a graphite crucible. Subsequently, the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier was added to the molten Al–20wt.% Si alloy, and the addition level of the modifier was 0.5wt.% for the base alloy. The molten metal was stirred at 850°C for approximately 5min to ensure adequate modification. In order to study the effect of holding time on primary silicon in the modified Al–Si alloy, the molten metal was poured into a metal mould (200mm×150mm×12mm) preheated at 150°C after holding for different times such as 5, 15, 30, 90, 180 and 240min. In addition, the effect of Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier on primary silicon has also been investigated by the addition of the modifier (0.5wt.%) to hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy. After cutting the samples from the unmodified and modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy at different holding times, the samples were etched with 5% NaOH solution. The morphological features of primary silicon in the unmodified and modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy at different holding times were characterized using scanning electron microscope (SEM). In order to reveal the state of La2O3 in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys at 850°C, Al powder (99.0 purity, ∼29μm) and 10wt.% La2O3 powder (99.5 purity, ∼0.05μm) were mixed for 6h and then pressed into cylindrical compacts (20mm diameter and about 15mm height). The cylindrical compact was heated to 850°C and then was held at the temperature for 1h. X-ray diffraction (XRD) was used to identify phase compositions of the sintered Al–10wt.% La2O3 compact. Brinell hardness was measured using a 1000kg load and 10mm diameter indenter (HB≤130), or a 750kg load and 5mm diameter indenter (HB>130), with a loading time of 30s. The average value of Brinell hardness was taken from five measurements.

Primary silicon (approximately average 150μm) in unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy exhibits coarse platelet, star-like and other irregular morphologies as shown in Fig. 1 . Figs. 2 and 3
show the effects of Al–P–Ti–TiC and Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifiers on primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy, respectively. It can be clearly observed that primary silicon modified with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier are smaller than that modified with Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier at different holding times (5, 30 and 90min, respectively). In the experiment, primary silicon size increased with the increase of holding time after the Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier was added to the melt of hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy as shown in Fig. 2. When the holding time is up to 90min, primary silicon exhibits irregular morphologies again as shown in Fig. 2(c), which indicates that the effect of the Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier on primary silicon has been badly weakened. On the contrary, it is interesting that after the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier was added to the melt of hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy, primary silicon size decrease with the increase in holding time from 5 to 30min, and the minimum primary silicon size (approximately average 18μm) can be obtained at the holding time of 30min (as shown in Fig. 3(c)). Subsequently, primary silicon size hardly change with the increase in holding time from 30 to 90min and then begin to gradually increase with the increase of holding time (90min after).

It is very important and significant to design a modifier to obtain the most effective modification of primary silicon. If the holding time is too short, the finest primary silicon size cannot be obtained. On the contrary, if the holding time is too long, the effectiveness of modification of primary silicon will be weakened. This weakened effectiveness at excessive holding time is well known as fading [18]. According to above experimental results, when the Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier was added to the melt of hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy, the holding time of the modifier should be very short, which should result from the speedy fading of the modifier. However, when the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier was added to the melt of hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy, the primary silicon size (approximately average 18μm) hardly change with the increase in holding time from 30 to 90min, which was mainly attributed to no fading of the modifier as shown in Fig. 4 . Subsequently, primary silicon size began to gradually increase (the holding time of 90min after), which was mainly attributed to the gradually fading of the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier as shown in Fig. 4. Therefore, it can be concluded that the best holding time of the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys is between 30 and 90min, and La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier plays an important role in determining modification mechanism of primary silicon.

In order to reveal the effect of La2O3 on primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys, it is necessary to study the state of La2O3 in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys. In the experiment, X-ray diffraction (XRD) was used to identify phase compositions of the sintered Al–10wt.% La2O3 compact. According to the result of XRD pattern, the unidentified phase and Al2O3 were detected but the corresponding reflections of La2O3 can not be observed in the XRD pattern as shown in Fig. 5 . Therefore, it can be deduced that La2O3 was decomposed (partly or completely) in the sintered Al–10wt.% La2O3 compact. According to the literature [19], CeO2 can be decomposed into [Ce] and [O] at high temperature and therefore, CeO2 exhibits the same function as Ce. When the modifier containing La2O3 was added to Al–20wt.% Si alloy at 850°C, La2O3 in the modifier was decomposed (partly or completely) and may generate the same function as La. Chang et al. [15] have examined the effect of mischmetal on the refinement of primary silicon phase and concluded that refinement of primary silicon was achieved with the addition of mischmetal to hypereutectic Al–21wt.% Si alloy. Some researcher suggested that RE metals cannot provide a suitable nucleation site of primary silicon, although some RE bearing compounds were observed around primary silicon. Lu and Hellawell [20] have proposed the mechanism of the impurity induced twinning in which atoms of the modifier are absorbed onto the growth steps of the silicon surface. A growth twin is created at the interface when the ratio (the atomic radius of the modification elements relative to that of silicon) is close to 1.646. The ratio of La atom radius (r) to silicon atom radius (r Si) is 1.59 [20]. Therefore, the atoms of La, due to the decomposition of La2O3, may be adsorbed upon the growth steps of silicon crystal and promote multiple twinning to cause structural modification [17]. Therefore, it may be understood that the variation of primary silicon size in modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier was mainly attributed to the decomposed quantities of La2O3. In other words, the decomposed quantities of La2O3 gradually increase with the increase in holding time from 5 to 30min and achieve the preferable quantities with the increase in holding time from 30 to 90min.

Table 2 shows the Brinell hardness values of the modified Al–20wt.% Si alloys (with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 and Al–P–Ti–TiC modifiers, respectively) at different holding times. For the modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier, Brinell hardness increase with the increase of holding time, and the maximum Brinell hardness value (140) is obtained at the holding time of 30min. Subsequently, Brinell hardness decrease with the increase of holding time (30min after). On the other hand, for the modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier, Brinell hardness decrease with the increase of holding time. Therefore, it can be concluded that the variation of Brinell hardness of hypereutectic Al–Si alloys mainly depend on the variation of primary silicon size. The Brinell hardness value of the unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy is 101. As compared with unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy, the Brinell hardness value of modified Al–20wt.% Si with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at the holding time of 30min is increased by 39%.

ce:list-item.ce:para: La2O3 in the Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier plays an important role in modifying primary silicon in hypereutectic Al–Si alloys.

The holding time of Al–P–Ti–TiC modifier in hypereutectic Al–20wt.% Si alloy should be very short, which should result from the speedy fading of the modifier. The minimum primary silicon size (approximately average 18μm) modified with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier is obtained at the holding time of 30min, and the primary silicon size hardly change with the increase in holding time from 30 to 90min.
As compared with unmodified Al–20wt.% Si alloy, the Brinell hardness value of modified Al–20wt.% Si alloy with Al–P–Ti–TiC–La2O3 modifier at the holding time of 30min is increased by 39%.

ce:acknowledgment.ce:para: This research is supported by Natural Science Foundation of China (No. 50501010) and the project 985-Automotive Engineering of Jilin University.

ce:other-ref.ce:textref: C.L. Xu, Q.C. Jiang, Y.F. Yang, H.Y. Wang, J.G. Wang, J. Alloys Compd., 2005, doi:10.1016/j.jallcom.2005.03.128.
